channel.title: The Brussels Times
channel.description: Latest posts from The Brussels Times.

item.title: Press association 'concerned' about journalists' visa situation ahead of World Cup
item.description: Iranian and African reporters face denied or limited US visas before the World Cup. AIPS calls the situation "unacceptable" and wants FIFA to intervene.

item.title: Airlines denounce passengers' compensation for delayed flights
item.description: Airlines say EU's delay policy punishes the many for the few as €8 billion costs mount. A reform plan now faces deadlock.

item.title: Agricultural giant France increasingly imports flour
item.description: Flour imports into France have soared 45% in two years, leaving millers warning of potential threats to domestic production and traditions.

item.title: Social Democrats oust ruling populists in EU Member State
item.description: Populist party Dawn over the Neman was removed from the ruling coalition as a centre-left ally joins instead.

item.title: US Secretary of Defence urges Europe to 'rearm' on D-Day anniversary
item.description: Comparing threats today to the D-Day invasion, the US defence chief urged Europe to counter ideological dangers landing on its shores. “Act decisively… or is it already too late?"

item.title: Ebola: nearly 500 cases confirmed
item.description: A rare strain of Ebola is driving a spike in cases across central Africa—over 470 infections and mounting deaths prompt global health warnings.

item.title: Forty-nine people die of dehydration after truck breaks down in the Sahara
US House of Representatives calls for an end to war with Iran
item.description: The US House of Representatives passed a resolution on Wednesday, by 215 votes to 208, calling for the withdrawal of US troops from the war with Iran.

item.title: Iran will respond 'decisively' to any attack, chief negotiator warns
item.description: Iran will respond decisively to any attack by the United States or Israel, Iranian Chief Negotiator Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf said in a statement on Wednesday.

item.title: Ebola: WHO reports 359 confirmed cases and 61 deaths
item.description: A total of 359 cases and 61 deaths have been confirmed in the latest outbreak involving the Bundibugyo variant of Ebola, according to the WHO.

item.title: Team of scientists extract yeast from prehistoric mummy to bake 'very good' bread
item.description: The team succeeded in producing a "very, very good" sourdough starter. they are now considering using the ancient yeast to brew beer.

item.title: Thousands in Belgian academia urge universities to sever ties with Israeli institutions
item.description: The group is calling for the immediate suspension of existing academic collaborations with Israeli institutions, as well as a moratorium on new partnership.

item.title: World's highest mountain Everest sees busiest climbing season on record
item.description: Everest can normally be climbed from both Nepal and China, although the northern route through China remained closed this year.

item.title: Trump administration dismantles key ocean observation network
item.description: US Government is dismantling a major oceanographic infrastructure network that has been studying coastal areas, marine ecosystems, and ocean currents for a decade.

item.title: War in the Middle East hits humanitarian aid supply chains
item.description: The global supply chain for humanitarian aid, disrupted by the conflict in the Middle East, may not return to normal until 2027, the United Nations warns.

item.title: Widespread condemnation of Russia's actions after drone crash in Romania
EU ministers to discuss Ebola outbreak in DRC in special video conference
item.description: The World Health Organisation (WHO) has declared a public health emergency of international concern, with cross-border risks requiring international coordination.

item.title: Belgium 'mostly silent' about Iran's protester executions, says dissident
item.description: "As with other European countries, Belgium has been totally sidelined. Its silence is the policy of appeasement,” says the exiled Ali Bagheri.

item.title: Gaza's journalists to receive international award
MSF workers undergo crash-course before deployment to Ebola-hit area
item.description: Some 25 Medecins Sans Frontieres staffers are following a two-day crash course on medical practices and protocols related to Ebola before heading to the DRC.
